Title: Auto-generated uniform controls
Summary: Every uniform you declare gets a UI control derived from its type + hint annotation: sliders for hint_range, native color pickers for source_color, grouped number inputs otherwise.
Body:
## What you get for each declaration

| Declaration                                            | Control                                          |
| ------------------------------------------------------ | ------------------------------------------------ |
| `uniform bool name;`                                   | Checkbox                                         |
| `uniform int name : hint_range(min, max[, step]);`     | Integer slider with min/max/step                 |
| `uniform float name : hint_range(min, max[, step]);`   | Float slider with min/max/step                   |
| `uniform int name;` / `uniform float name;`            | Plain number input                               |
| `uniform vec3 name : source_color;`                    | Native browser color picker                      |
| `uniform vec4 name : source_color;`                    | Color picker + separate alpha slider             |
| `uniform vec2/3/4 name;` (no hint)                     | Grouped number inputs, one per component         |
| `uniform sampler2D name;`                              | AssetPicker — pick any image in the Godot project |

`hint_color` is treated as an alias for `source_color` — same control either way.

## sampler2D specifics

Each user-declared `sampler2D` uniform gets its own AssetPicker; the runtime allocates a dedicated WebGL2 texture unit per sampler (unit 0 stays reserved for the built-in `TEXTURE` / `u_texture`). When you swap an image, the runtime tears down the old texture for that sampler and uploads the new one to the same unit, so the shader keeps sampling from the same name without re-linking.

Until you've picked an image, the sampler is **unbound** — sampling from it returns zero. Most shaders that use auxiliary samplers (noise maps, gradient ramps, palette LUTs) won't render usefully until you pick something; the test image picker below the canvas only fills `TEXTURE`, not extra samplers.

**Sampler hints affect texture parameters:**

- `filter_nearest` / `filter_linear` (+ the mipmap variants) drive `TEXTURE_MIN/MAG_FILTER` when the image uploads. NEAREST is the default (good for pixel art); LINEAR matters when the shader does sub-pixel UV sampling (warps, lens distortion, hallucinations).
- `repeat_enable` / `repeat_disable` drive `TEXTURE_WRAP_S/T`. CLAMP_TO_EDGE is default; REPEAT tiles the texture across the UV range.
- `hint_screen_texture` on a sampler **suppresses the AssetPicker** for that uniform — instead, it's aliased to whatever's in the main test-image picker above. Lets you author post-process shaders against Godot's `uniform sampler2D SCREEN_TEXTURE : hint_screen_texture;` syntax and preview them against a representative screenshot without changing the source.
- `hint_previous_frame` on a sampler **also suppresses the AssetPicker** and is wired automatically to a ping-pong back-FBO holding the last frame's rendered output. Lets you author iterative effects like trails, decay, feedback blur. Bleepforge-specific hint; Godot users need to set up SubViewport ping-pong manually for deployment. The first frame samples zero (back-FBO is freshly cleared); trails build up over the next few frames.

Multiple hints stack via comma syntax: `uniform sampler2D PREV : hint_previous_frame, filter_linear;`.

## Default values

The default value from the declaration (`= 0.5`, `= vec3(1.0, 0.5, 0.0)`, `= true`) seeds the initial control state. `vec(...)` literals are parsed component-by-component; `vec3(0.5)` expands to `[0.5, 0.5, 0.5]` (Godot's broadcast shorthand).

If the declaration has `hint_range` but no `=`, the slider seeds at the range's `min`. Without either, the control starts at type-appropriate zero (0 / false / [0, 0, 0]).

## Preservation across edits

When you change the source — add a new uniform, rename one, change a hint — the panel re-derives its shape on the next parse. Tweaked values are **preserved across the change** when a uniform's name *and* type both still match. If you add a brand-new uniform, it seeds from its default. If you rename one, the old name's value drops and the new name starts from default.

In practice this means you can scrub a slider value, then go back to the editor and add a new uniform, and your scrubbed value doesn't reset.

## Reset

The **Reset uniforms** link at the bottom of the controls reseeds every uniform from its current declaration's default. Doesn't reload from disk; doesn't touch the source. Just the in-memory values.

## Limitations

- `mat2/3/4` uniforms aren't in the v1 subset. If you need matrices in a shader you preview live, you'd be coming from a `spatial` context anyway, which has its own subset blockers — see [the translator subset](/help/shaders/translator-subset).
- Custom hint types (`hint_default_white`, `hint_normal`, etc.) on the supported scalar/vector types are rejected — they're 3D / sampler-affecting hints that don't apply to the canvas_item live preview.
